title: After Trimming Net Loss to $4.9M, Newegg Guides for 2026 Profit - Profitability Inflection Point
text:
Newegg Commerce 2026 Guidance and Profitability Inflection Analysis.

FY2025 Net Loss: The company achieved a dramatic reduction in net losses to just $4.9 million from $43.3 million in FY2024, representing an 89% improvement in losses.

2026 Profitability Guidance: For fiscal year 2026, Newegg projects a return to profitability with net income guidance of $6.1 million to $15.7 million. This represents a critical inflection point if achieved.

Path to Profitability: The company achieved positive adjusted EBITDA of $24.8 million in FY2025 (vs negative $9.5M in FY2024), demonstrating strong cash generation capabilities. This provides confidence that bottom-line GAAP profitability is achievable.

Key Enablers: Revenue growth of 16.9% combined with gross margin expansion. Operating expense discipline and SG&A reductions. Marketplace growth improving margin profile. GPU and AI-driven demand providing tailwind.

Valuation Implications: If 2026 profitability targets are achieved, trading multiples could expand significantly. Even at modest 15x P/E on $10M net income would imply $150M profit value vs current $467M market cap, suggesting undervaluation.

Risk Factors: Execution risk on profitability targets. GPU market normalization impact. Competitive dynamics with larger retailers. Macroeconomic slowdown risk.

Catalyst Timeline: Q1 2026 earnings due in May 2026 will be critical early test of profitability trajectory. H1 2026 earnings in August 2026 will validate sustainability.
